$comment: D-CSUBSET-NEG-INT-TO-PTR-SIGN-EXTEND: a signed narrower int->pointer conversion must sign-extend to pointer width (the universal C convention). A volatile-seeded runtime `int -1` -> `void*` yields an all-ones pointer; the HIGH 32 bits (observed via a 64-bit-on-EVERY-target `unsigned long long` -- NOT `unsigned long`, which is 32-bit on Windows LLP64 and would truncate the bits under test into a vacuous witness) are set iff sign-extended. exit 20 on EVERY target: sign-extension is target-INDEPENDENT (only sqlite's arm64 >4 GiB address layout turned this value bug into a runtime failure). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the combineCast widen -> zero-extend -> (v>>32)==0 -> exit 10. The `release` arm proves ConstFold does not mis-fold the extension (it folds neither SExt/ZExt nor IntToPtr).
